title: Weak SSL/TLS protocols should be avoided
description: Encryption algorithms are essential for protecting sensitive information and ensuring secure communications in a variety of domains. They are used for several important reasons: Confidentiality, privacy, and intellectual property protection Security during transmission or on storage devices Data integrity, general trust, and authentication When selecting encryption algorithms, tools, or combinations, you should also consider two things: No encryption is unbreakable. The strength of an encryption algorithm is usually measured by the effort required to crack it within a reasonable time frame.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Weak SSL/TLS protocols should be avoided. Using deprecated SSL/TLS protocol versions (SSLv2, SSLv3, TLSv1.0, TLSv1.1) exposes connections to known vulnerabilities like POODLE, BEAST, and others. Use TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 for secure communications.
